info.title: Oore CI API
info.description:
REST API for Oore CI — a self-hosted, Flutter-first mobile CI and internal app distribution platform.

The backend daemon (`oored`) exposes this API on the configured listen address. All endpoints under `/v1/` use JSON request/response bodies unless noted otherwise.

## Authentication

- **Setup endpoints** (`/v1/setup/*`) are token-gated by a bootstrap session token and auto-disabled after setup completes.
- **Auth endpoints** (`/v1/auth/*`) support local-mode login and OIDC login/logout flows.
- **All other endpoints** require a valid session token via `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header.
- **Runner endpoints** use a separate runner token for authentication.

## Base URL

Since Oore CI is self-hosted, the base URL is your daemon's listen address (e.g. `http://localhost:8787`).

get.description: Returns `{"ok": true}` when the daemon is running.

Downloads an artifact using a scoped download token. No session auth required —
the token itself is the authorization. For S3/R2 storage, redirects to a presigned URL.
Returns an Apple property-list manifest authorized by the scoped token in the URL.

Returns all API tokens visible to the caller. Admins and owners see all
tokens; other roles see only their own.

post.description:
Creates a new API token. The plaintext token is returned only in this
response and cannot be retrieved again.

delete.description:
Revokes an API token by ID. Non-admin users can only revoke their own
tokens.
Revokes a scoped download token so it can no longer be used.

post.summary: List available artifacts for a bounded set of builds

post.description: Returns a signed download URL for an artifact. URLs expire after 15 minutes.

Creates a one-hour install session for an APK or install-ready signed IPA.
QA viewers may use this endpoint through their artifact read permission.

412.description: External HTTPS access is not ready

post.description:
Generates a scoped, time-limited download token for a specific artifact.
The token can be shared with external users who don't have an account.

get.description: Lists all scoped download tokens for a specific artifact, including expired and revoked ones.

Returns a paginated, filterable list of audit log entries recording
user and system actions. Owner and admin roles only.

post.description:
Creates a loopback-only local session without OIDC.
If setup is still pending in Local Only mode, first login auto-finalizes
local owner bootstrap. When setup is already complete, loopback local
login remains available even if External Access is enabled.

403.description: Blocked by mode policy or non-loopback source

post.description: Invalidates the current session.
Exchanges the authorization code for tokens and creates a session.

requestBody.description: Authorization code and state from OIDC provider

get.description:
Initiates the OIDC authorization code flow for user login.
Only available after setup is complete.

post.description:
Creates a session from trusted proxy identity headers when remote auth
mode is configured to trusted proxy.

403.description: Untrusted peer, disabled user, or user missing

get.description: Returns builds, optionally filtered by project, pipeline, or status.

post.description: Cancels a queued or running build.

get.description: Returns paginated historical build logs.

Server-sent event stream of build logs. Requires a short-lived streaming
token obtained from `POST /v1/builds/{build_id}/stream-token`.

post.description: Creates a new build cloning the original build's config_snapshot, branch, and commit_sha.

409.description: Source build is not in a terminal state

post.description:
Exchanges a session token for a short-lived (5 min) streaming token
suitable for use in SSE query parameters.

Exchanges a short-lived, single-use code created by `oore frontend invite`
for the backend trusted-proxy proof and configured identity header. This
endpoint accepts requests only from configured trusted-proxy peer CIDRs.

401.description: Invalid, expired, or consumed pairing code

409.description: Backend setup or trusted proxy configuration is incomplete

get.summary: Fetch a private GitLab repository avatar through Oore

get.description: Returns all SCM integrations, optionally filtered by provider.

post.description: Finalises the GitHub App manifest flow after the redirect back from GitHub.
Returns a URL to navigate the browser to for GitHub App manifest creation.
Creates a GitLab integration with OAuth or personal token auth.

get.summary: Browse local directories for local repository registration

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Absolute directory path to browse. Defaults to the daemon user's home directory

post.description:
- **GitHub**: Fetches GitHub App installations and syncs their repositories.
- **GitLab**: Refreshes accessible projects for linked accounts and syncs them as repositories.
Validates a pipeline configuration without persisting it.

put.description: Upload keystores and configure debug/release signing profiles.

Upload certificates, provisioning profiles, and API credentials.
Request body limit: 10 MiB.

post.description: Registers a device UDID and optionally triggers a provisioning profile sync.
Re-downloads provisioning profiles from Apple via App Store Connect API.

get.summary: List available artifacts across a project

post.description: Queues a new build for the specified project.

409.description: Project source is not configured or resolvable

get.summary: Preview a Markdown changelog for a manual build.

post.summary: Add a member to a project

400.description: Invalid user or user already has implicit access

delete.summary: Remove a member from a project

get.description:
Reads supported Oore workflow files from the linked repository without
modifying it. The response contains validated behavior but never raw YAML,
environment values, or source credentials.

parameters.name: ref
parameters.description: Branch, tag, or commit; defaults to the project branch

get.summary: Get project retention (effective policy merged with overrides)

delete.summary: Delete project retention override (revert to global)

get.description:
Returns the current setup state and instance ID. This endpoint is
always public and contains no sensitive information.

post.description: Registers a new build runner and returns a runner authentication token.

get.description: Retrieves a single runner by ID, including its current status and health information.

post.description: Runner claims the next queued build. Returns `null` job if no builds are available.
Sends a heartbeat from a runner to report its status. Authenticated via runner token.

get.description: Runner checks the current status of its assigned build.

post.description: Runner creates an artifact record and gets a signed upload URL.

get.summary: Discover a private GitLab repository for checkout
get.description:
Internal Git smart-HTTP endpoint. The runner token is accepted only for
the repository assigned to this job; GitLab credentials remain server-side.

403.description: Repository path does not match the assigned job

post.summary: Stream a private GitLab upload-pack request

403.description: Repository path does not match the assigned job

post.description: Runner appends log chunks to a build. Max 10,000 lines per build, 4KB per line.
Runner reports build status transitions and step results.

put.description: Configure the storage backend for build artifacts (local, S3, or R2).

get.description: Returns effective public URL and allowed frontend origins used by External Access checks.

put.description: Owner-only update for public URL and allowed frontend origins.

get.summary: Get current OIDC configuration for External Access
get.description:
Returns the current runtime OIDC provider configuration (issuer, client ID,
discovered endpoints). Never exposes the client secret.

put.description:
Owner-only endpoint to configure runtime OIDC after setup is complete.
Performs provider discovery and stores issuer/client settings.

400.description: Invalid input or OIDC discovery failure

409.description: Setup state does not allow runtime OIDC configuration

post.description:
Owner-only endpoint to test OIDC provider discovery without committing changes.
Validates that the issuer URL is reachable and returns discovered endpoints.

400.description: Invalid input or OIDC discovery failure

get.description: Returns check-by-check readiness required before enabling External Access (`runtime_mode=remote`).
Returns all configured notification channels.

post.description: Creates a new webhook or Mattermost notification channel.

get.description: Returns details for a single notification channel.

put.description: Updates an existing notification channel. Omitted fields are preserved.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a notification channel and its delivery history.

get.description: Returns delivery history for a notification channel (most recent 100).

post.description: Sends a test notification to verify the channel is configured correctly.

put.description: Update instance-wide preferences (e.g. key storage mode).

400.description: External Access preflight failed or unsupported values

403.description: Owner-only mode change attempted by non-owner

post.description:
Exchanges a one-time bootstrap token (generated by the CLI) for a
setup session token. Rate-limited: 5 failed attempts per token hash
triggers lockout.

Finalises the setup flow, transitions state to `ready`, inserts the
owner into the users table, and clears the setup session.
Creates the setup owner without OIDC when runtime mode is `local`.

Performs OIDC discovery on the provided issuer URL and stores the
provider configuration. Requires a valid setup session.

post.summary: Claim owner identity from trusted proxy headers
post.description:
Creates the owner record from trusted proxy identity headers after the
proxy-authenticated email matches the configured setup owner email.

200.description: Owner created from trusted proxy identity

401.description: Invalid setup session or missing/invalid identity header

403.description: Not in trusted-proxy mode, untrusted proxy peer, or owner email mismatch

post.description:
Initiates the OIDC authorization code flow to create the instance owner.
Returns an authorization URL for the frontend to redirect to.

Completes the setup OIDC flow by exchanging the authorization code
for tokens and creating the owner account.
Stores setup-time runtime mode and remote auth mode before owner creation.

409.description: Setup already complete or owner already created

get.description:
Returns a summary of current setup configuration. Requires a valid
setup session.

post.summary: Configure trusted proxy auth during setup
post.description:
Upserts trusted proxy settings for remote trusted-proxy mode, including
the expected initial owner email.

409.description: Setup already complete or owner already created

get.description:
Reports whether the backend is managed by the supported macOS launchd
service and whether an update is currently running. Requires owner role.

post.description:
Runs the installed updater and hands restart control to the managed
macOS launchd service. Requires owner role.

409.description: Managed updater unavailable or already running

get.description: Returns all users. Requires `owner` or `admin` role.

post.description: Creates a new user with `invited` status. Requires `owner` or `admin` role.

get.description: Returns the profile of the currently authenticated user.

delete.description: Soft-deletes a user. Requires `owner` or `admin` role.

post.description: Re-enables a previously disabled user.
Creates a short-lived session for an active QA Viewer. Owner only.

400.description: Target is not a QA Viewer

patch.description: Changes a user's role. Requires `owner` or `admin` role.

post.description:
Receives push/PR events from GitHub and triggers matching pipelines.
Authenticated via webhook signature verification.

Receives push/MR events from GitLab and triggers matching pipelines.
Authenticated via webhook secret token.

token.description: The plaintext token. Only returned on creation, never again.

platforms.description: Optional one-run platform selection. Omitted builds every platform configured by the pipeline.

events.description: Terminal build statuses to notify on. Empty or omitted means all terminal events.

secret.description: HMAC secret for signing webhook payloads (webhook only, optional).

oneOf.description: SMTP configuration (required for email channel type).

url.description: Webhook/Mattermost incoming webhook URL (required for webhook/mattermost).

single_use.description: If true, token is consumed after first download.

ttl_secs.description: TTL in seconds (default: 86400 = 24 hours, max: 604800 = 7 days).

reference.description: Branch, tag, or commit requested for discovery.

truncated.description: True when more matching files existed than the bounded response could inspect.

redirect_url.description: Frontend URL to redirect to after GitHub App creation completes.

create_url.description: URL to navigate the browser to — serves an auto-submitting form that POSTs the manifest to GitHub.

events.description: Which terminal build statuses trigger this channel. Empty means all.

has_secret.description: True if the channel has an HMAC secret configured (webhook only).

has_smtp_config.description: True if the channel has SMTP configuration (email only).

has_url.description: True if the channel has a URL configured (the actual URL is never exposed).

RepositoryWorkflowExecutionPreview.description: Pipeline behavior safe to show before import. Environment values are never returned.

RepositoryWorkflowPreview.description: Secret-free representation of a repository-owned pipeline config.

secret.description: New HMAC secret. Omit to keep existing; pass empty string to clear.

oneOf.description: Partial SMTP configuration update (email only). Omit fields to keep existing.

url.description: New URL. Omit to keep existing.

tags.name: Setup
tags.description: Initial instance setup flow (bootstrap token → mode-aware owner creation). Auto-disabled after setup completes.

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: Mode-aware authentication and session management. Enabled only after setup is complete.

tags.name: Users
tags.description: User management — invite, list, update roles, disable/re-enable.

tags.name: Instance Settings
tags.description: Instance-wide configuration — artifact storage, key storage preferences.

tags.name: Retention Policy
tags.description: Build retention and cleanup — automatic cleanup of old builds and artifacts based on age, count, or size policies.

tags.name: Integrations
tags.description: SCM integrations — local git, GitHub App, and GitLab.

tags.name: Projects
tags.description: Project CRUD — each project groups one or more pipelines.

tags.name: Project Members
tags.description: Per-project role assignments for granular RBAC.

tags.name: Pipelines
tags.description: Pipeline configuration — build platforms, commands, triggers, concurrency.

tags.name: Pipeline Signing
tags.description: Code signing configuration — Android keystores, iOS certificates/profiles.

tags.name: Builds
tags.description: Build lifecycle — queue, list, detail, cancel.

tags.name: Runners
tags.description: Build runner management — register, heartbeat, job claim/status.

tags.name: Build Logs
tags.description: Build log ingestion and retrieval — append, paginated fetch, SSE streaming.

tags.name: Artifacts
tags.description: Build artifact management — upload, list, download via signed URLs.

tags.name: Notification Channels
tags.description: Outbound notification channel configuration — webhook, Mattermost.

tags.name: Audit Logs
tags.description: Read-only audit trail of all user and system actions.

tags.name: API Tokens
tags.description: API token management — create, list, and revoke tokens for programmatic access.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Incoming webhook receivers for GitHub and GitLab.
